description_long: This object is a large, tube-shaped anti-armor weapon resembling a classic rocket-propelled grenade launcher, such as the RPG-7. It features a long cylindrical barrel with a flared muzzle at one end and a narrower breech at the other. Two prominent pistol-style grips are attached beneath the barrel, one near the rear and one closer to the center, providing stability and control. Iron sights are mounted on the top, with a rear sight near the breech and a front sight near the muzzle. The surface is metallic, with a dark, matte finish and subtle texturing, giving it a robust, utilitarian appearance. The overall construction suggests a combination of steel and polymer components, designed for durability and field use.
description: A large, tube-shaped anti-armor weapon with a flared muzzle, dual pistol grips, and iron sights, finished in dark matte metal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, flared muzzle, both pistol grips, and iron sights clearly.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, flared muzzle opening and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the barrel, grips, and sight mounts.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the breech end, rear sight, and the alignment of the grips along the barrel.
